title: Cinematic and Web Funnel Engine — PROCESS-CERTIFICATE.json (Skill 62, U20)
description: The P16-CERTIFY signed certificate (spec Sections 16, 17.9). Emitted by scripts/prove_certificate.py ONLY when every one of the 16 upstream phase gates (P0-ENVIRONMENT..P15-PRODUCTION) independently re-evaluates PASS on the SAME run_dir and the run-scoped front-door nonce (ADR-6) is present. This schema validates SHAPE only — semantic rules (all 17 phases actually contiguous 0..16, every status literally 'PASS', all_phases_pass literally true, the HMAC signature verifying against a supplied nonce) are enforced in code by prove_certificate.verify()/evaluate(), matching this skill's existing doctrine (see structure/deployment-receipt.schema.json and prove_deployment.py's own docstring: schema validity is a DIFFERENT, structural pass from the semantic re-derivation a prove_*.py performs).

certificate.description: Discriminator so a certificate from a different skill/schema can never be mistaken for this one.

run_id.description: The locked project-manifest.json project_id when one exists under run_dir, else the run_dir's own basename.

nonce_fingerprint.description: sha256(run-scoped front-door nonce)[:16] hex. Binds the certificate to one specific front-door run WITHOUT ever storing the raw nonce value in an artifact (spec 20: secrets/sensitive material by reference only, never logged in full).

phases.description: One entry per phase P0-ENVIRONMENT..P16-CERTIFY, contiguous order 0..16. A valid, unsigned-tamper-free certificate has every entry's status == 'PASS' and all_phases_pass == true; the enum below permits the other values ONLY so this same schema can also validate an in-progress or rejected aggregation snapshot (e.g. certificate-status.json) passed through the same shape-checker before its semantic verdict is read.

artifact_index.description: The 'final artifact index' spec Section 16 requires for P16 — every P0-P15 produces_artifact filename that was actually found under run_dir at certification time, with a content hash so a later restart/redeploy or independent audit can prove nothing shifted underneath the signed certificate.

signature.description: hmac_sha256(key=run-scoped front-door nonce, message=canonical JSON of every OTHER field, sort_keys + tight separators).hexdigest().
